data.title: Textron declares $0.02 dividend
data.description:
• Textron (NYSE:TXT) declared $0.02/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 0.09%
  • Payable Jan. 1; for shareholders of record Dec. 13; ex-div Dec. 13.
  • See TXT Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Enterprise software stocks move mostly down ahead of earnings
data.description:
ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) kicks off the earnings season for enterprise software companies today, but most stocks in the sector were trending down by afternoon trading.

ServiceNow had slipped 1%. A consensus of analysts expects the IT service management company to report earnings per share of $3.45 on revenue of $2.75B after the markets close.

IBM (NYSE:IBM) also reports after the bell tonight. Shares of Big Blue were relatively static. Wall Street estimates IBM to report earnings per share of $2.23 on revenue of $15.08B for the quarter.

Salesforce (NYSE:CRM), one of the strongest names in the customer relationship management sector, was down 1%. It reveals its latest quarterly report post-market on November 27.

Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) was about 0.5% lower. SAP (NYSE:SAP), which released its financial report earlier this week, was an outlier and ticked up 1%. Intuit (NASDAQ:INTU), Workday (NASDAQ:WDAY) and Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) all slid 1%.

Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), which releases its earnings on October 30, was down less than 1%. Atlassian (NASDAQ:TEAM), which reports the following day on October 31, had dropped the most among this group at 3%.

data.title: Medpace cut to neutral by Baird, slowed revenue growth cited
data.description:
Baird downgraded Medpace (NASDAQ:MEDP) to neutral, citing slowed near-term revenue growth.

Baird said that while 2025 may see low single digit to mid-single digit revenue growth, if bookings recover in the second half of 2025, “then 2026 could easily return to double-digit percentage growth, just like 2018-2019 exhibited.”

The investment firm also noted that Medpace “posts its best margin in slow growth environments and typically outperforms cautionary tales.” It added that the strongest headwind to margin will be hiring when growth accelerates.

Baird lowered its price target to $349 from $413.

data.title: Intercontinental Exchange records record trading activity across its interest rate markets
data.description:
Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE:ICE) said Wednesday it recorded record trading activity across its interest rate derivatives markets in Q3 2024, with over 190M contracts traded, exceeding the previous record set in Q1 2020.

The company's interest rate markets also reached record average daily volume ("ADV") during the three-month period with 2.9M contracts traded, up 39% since the start of the year.

“As participants manage changing interest rate policy and exposure between central bank meeting dates, we are seeing heightened trading activity which is fueling liquidity," said Caterina Caramaschi, vice president, Financial Derivatives at ICE.

ICE offers customers liquid, multi-currency markets to trade interest rate benchmarks Euribor, SONIA and SARON, as well as Gilts, the benchmark for the U.K. government bond yield curve.

Last week, ICE said it saw record trading activity across total futures and options market during Q3 2024, with 517M contracts traded during the quarter.

data.title: S&P 500 slips for a third straight day amid earnings deluge; all eyes on Tesla results
data.description:
Wall Street's benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) on Wednesday fell for a third straight day, weighed down by consumer discretionary stocks and a post-earnings plunge in some high-profile companies.

Meanwhile, a continued bond sell-off on bets that the Federal Reserve would not be as aggressive with rate cuts as previously expected also weighed on equities.

The S&P (SP500) was last -0.51% at 5,821.61 points in midday trade, and was on track to post a three-day losing streak for the first time since early September. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.74% at 18,436.51 points, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -0.65% at 42,645.49 points.

Of the 11 S&P sectors, eight were in the red. Consumer Discretionary was -1.6%, weighed down by a -5.4% retreat in McDonald's (MCD).

The world's largest fast-food chain has been linked to an E. coli outbreak by the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention that has sickened 49 people across ten states and killed one person. The Dow 30 component was also the primary drag on the blue-chip index.

Boeing (BA) was -2.5% and was a top percentage loser on the Dow (DJI) as well. The troubled planemaker reported a whopping $6B in quarterly losses due to a crippling machinists' strike, and top boss Kelly Ortberg called for a "fundamental culture change."

Additionally, Coca-Cola (KO) was -2.2% and put pressure on the Dow (DJI). The world's largest soft drinks company delivered a solid jump in quarterly organic revenue, but kept its full-year profit guidance unchanged despite higher prices.

Among other earnings-related moves, Enphase Energy (ENPH) plunged -13.4% after the solar equipment maker announced weaker-than-anticipated results.

All eyes are on Tesla (TSLA), with the electric vehicle giant slated to report earnings after the closing bell. For all the hype generated by Tesla's (TSLA) recent robotaxi reveal, investors will still be looking at the metrics of its core electric vehicle business, including the operating margin line. The Magnificent 7 member has significantly underperformed its peers in terms of stock gains.

Turning to the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields advanced as a bond sell-off stretched into yet another day. Traders have been steadily recalibrating their expectations for aggressive Fed rate cuts following strong economic data recently. Comments from well-known Fed speakers this week have expressed a preference for gradual policy easing.

The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was up 1 basis point to 4.23%, while the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) was up 3 basis points to 4.07%.

According to Goldman Sachs FICC and Equities, the rise in the US10Y is also "part of the 'Trump Trade' as GS research points out a red sweep will see a higher 10yr."

"When do equities notice? Historically, a 2 SD move in (the US10Y), equivalent to around 60 bps today (3yr lookback), over a month is when impingement for equity markets kicks in. Given we've moved 46 bps MTD, this simple rule of thumb argues that a move towards 4.30% in where things would get tricky for stocks. Speed of the move matters and keep an eye on real yields," Goldman Sachs FICC and Equities added.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

Looking at Wednesday's economic calendar, the National Association of Realtors said existing home sales slipped 1% in September to a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 3.84M. That marked the slowest pace since 2010, when the housing market was struggling with the aftereffects of the housing bust and the Great Recession.

There was also data from the Atlanta Fed, which indicated that firms' year-ahead inflation expectations in October remained relatively unchanged at 2.2%.

The Fed's Beige Book on U.S. economic activity will be released later in the afternoon.

More on the markets

  • Paul Tudor Jones on inflation hedges: Long gold, bitcoin, commodities
  • Are You Looking For Data To Support Your Views?
  • Latest Memo From Howard Marks: Ruminating On Asset Allocation
  • Rising Dividend Outlook Boosting Stock Prices
  • Soaring $1-handle stocks leaping into IBKR’s most-active list may point to market froth

data.title: Discover Financial ticks lower on report Capital One deal probed by New York AG
data.description:
Discover Financial (NYSE:DFS) quickly ticked down 0.6% after a report that the New York attorney general is probing its planned sale to Capital One (COF). Capital One also edged lower by 0.6%.

New York Attorney General Letitia James is seeking court permission to issue subpoenas as part of an antitrust review of the deal, according to a Bloomberg report on Wednesday.

James said New York could be especially impacted by the transaction because Capital One (COF) and Discover (DFS) have over $9.5 billion and $6.5 billion in credit card loans in the state, according to the report.

Developing story ...

data.title: NextEra 'very interested' in restarting shuttered Iowa nuclear plant
data.description:
NextEra Energy (NYSE:NEE) said Wednesday it is conducting engineering studies and speaking with U.S. regulators about the possible restart of its closed Duane Arnold nuclear power plant in Iowa.

"We're very interested in recommissioning the plant," Chairman, President and CEO John Ketchum said on NextEra's (NEE) earnings conference call, adding the site uses older technology which may be simpler than other systems to revive.

"That gives us optimism about being able to do this at an attractive price," Ketchum said.

Rival Constellation Energy announced last month that it plans to restart the Three Mile Island nuclear plant in Pennsylvania to supply Microsoft data centers.

Even so, bringing the 600 MW nuclear plant back into service would be costly with no guarantee the economics of the project would be justified, according to Jefferies analyst Julien Dumoulin-Smith.

"We believe a Duane Arnold restart is challenging, given the plant damage and robust regional wind generation," Dumoulin-Smith said.

NextEra Energy (NEE) shares trade little changed after the utility compared reported mixed Q3 results, beating earnings estimates but missing on revenues.

data.title: Chewy's competition builds, putting its premium positioning at risk
data.description:
Chewy (NYSE:CHWY) has been a significant beneficiary of e-commerce penetration over the past decade, and its growth trajectory has proved to be more resilient than its peers in the pet category. But while Chewy (CHWY) benefits from a highly recurring customer, the company still faces tough competition from players like Amazon (AMZN) and Walmart (WMT) putting its “premium positioning” at risk.

Accordingly, Needham’s analyst team led by Bernie McTernan gives the stock a Hold rating.

Chewy can compete with names like Amazon (AMZN), Walmart (WMT) as well as Petco Health and Wellness Company (WOOF) because of their 16 fulfillment centers which allows them to reach 80% of their customers overnight, and 100% within two days. The company also benefits from a very high autoship revenue base, creating leverage on their fixed infrastructure investments.

However, the risk of a deteriorating consumer environment (which adversely impacts highly discretionary spending on pets) and the potential that the vet clinic investment fails to move the needle could damage Chewy’s (CHWY) margin expansion potential.

Analysts are mixed on Chewy (CHWY) with Seeking Alpha authors rating the stock as a Hold, while Wall Street analysts view Chewy (CHWY) as a Buy. Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating gives the stock a Hold rating.

Shares of Chewy (CHWY) are down 2% matched by a 2% loss for Petco Wellness (WOOF).

data.title: Roche CEO says antitrust regulators should block Catalent sale to Novo Holdings (update)
data.description:
Update 12:30pm: Adds Catalent response.

The CEO of Swiss pharmaceuticals behemoth Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) said he believes antitrust regulators should not allow Novo Holdings to purchase Catalent (NYSE:CTLT).

"It's not a problem for us, but it could be a problem for other smaller players, if there is a restriction in how many (contract manufacturers) are available," Roche CEO Thomas Schinecker said on a media call, according to a Reuters report on Wednesday. "From an industry perspective, it would be a wrong decision by authorities."

The Roche CEO comments come as some other industry players, including Eli Lilly (LLY), Novo Nordisk's main competitor in the GLP-1 space, have reportedly come out against the Catalent (CTLT) deal.

"We are confident that the transaction is pro-competitive, and we are unaware of any competitive GLP-1 products being manufactured for commercial sale at the three sites that Novo Nordisk is planning to acquire," a Catalent spokesperson said in a statement. "We have and will continue to work closely with the FTC and EU regulators as intended under the law. We still expect the transaction to close toward the end of the calendar year, subject to customary closing conditions."

Last week, several consumer groups said that the Federal Trade Commission should block the planned $16.5 billion deal, arguing that a deal would hurt competition in weigh-loss drugs.

Schinecker later clarified his comments on Roche's results call on Wednesday.

"I was asked around manufacturing for our, specifically the CT388 because the oral molecule is a small molecule," Schinecker said on the results call. "So we have all of the manufacturing capacity that we need. And here we have agreements with CDMOs and, or CMOs and yeah, and we will continue to build up our own manufacturing. Now, I think in general, if companies start buying up CMOs, that will limit the amount of competition that there can be, given that there are also a lot of smaller companies that may not have as much access to CMOs as we have. And that's where my comment came from. And I stand behind that. I think it doesn't make sense from a perspective from antitrust authorities who wants competition."
In February, Catalent said it had agreed to be acquired by Novo Holdings for $63.50 a share. Novo Holdings will then sell three Catalent manufacturing sites to Novo Nordisk (NVO), which should help increase production of the company’s hugely popular weight-loss drug, Wegovy.

data.title: Quest upgraded to outperform by Baird, sector momentum cited
data.description:
• Baird upgraded Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX) to outperform, citing sector momentum.
  • “With favorable sector trends, including stable pricing, core volume growth, heightened deal prospects, no PAMA, and LDT fears long faded, we see safety and growth,” said Baird, in a note.
  • The investment firm also noted that if the Federal Reserve ends up embarking on a “prolonged rate cut path, labs historically (notably) outperform in those periods.”
  • Baird raised its price target to $182 from $157.
